headline_summary: Iran war-related incidents expand into Gulf states: drones hit Kuwait’s Mina al-Ahmadi refinery and a power/water plant; U.S. aircraft are downed over Iran/Gulf.
Reuters reports U.S. intelligence sees Iran unlikely to ease its effective Strait of Hormuz chokehold soon, sustaining disruption to a key oil chokepoint.

Diplomacy accelerates: UK-led talks among ~40 countries explore a coalition to reopen Hormuz; UN Security Council vote planned on Bahrain-drafted shipping protection resolution with China opposing force authorization.

Selective transit/assurances emerge even as disruption persists (Philippines says Iran promised safe passage for Philippine-flagged ships; CMA CGM vessel transits Hormuz after signaling ‘Owner France’).
Oil market structure signals acute near-term tightness (record WTI prompt backwardation) while OPEC+ weighs further output hikes contingent on reopening.
Regional spillover continues: Israel strikes Beirut; U.S. embassy warns Iran-aligned groups may target Lebanese universities.
risk_drivers: Effective closure/constraint of the Strait of Hormuz and uncertainty over reopening timeline
Kinetic spillover to Gulf infrastructure (Kuwait refinery/utilities drone attacks)
U.S.-Iran air incidents (downed aircraft, rescue operations) increasing escalation/retaliation risk
Shipping insurance/freight dislocations and potential Iranian permit/fee regime for transit
Spillover into Lebanon/Israel theatre with heightened strike risk

Energy: oil_up_risk: elevated risk premium while Hormuz remains constrained; freight/insurance costs reinforce higher delivered prices
Refined product volatility risk if Gulf refining/utilities attacks recur (e.g., Mina al-Ahmadi incident)
Benchmark dislocations (Dubai/Oman/Murban deliverability issues) can raise hedging basis risk and margin calls
FX: em_fx_pressure: higher energy import bills can pressure EM current accounts and local inflation
risk_off_flows: flight-to-quality can support USD/JPY/CHF if escalation widens
Rates: rates_credit_spreads: geopolitics-driven inflation risk can lift term premia while widening credit spreads under risk-off
Headline risk can raise rate volatility and swap spread volatility around major diplomatic/military updates
Equities: risk_off_flows: broader equity drawdown risk on renewed strikes/shipping disruption; relative support for energy/defense vs transports
Higher input/fuel costs pressure consumer and industrial margins; shipping/airlines face route and insurance risks

sources.title: Exclusive: US intelligence warns Iran unlikely to ease Hormuz Strait chokehold soon, sources say
Downed planes raise new perils for Trump as Tehran hunts for missing US pilot
Drones hit Kuwait's Mina al-Ahmadi refinery, fires reported, no injuries
Britain says 40 countries discuss reopening Strait of Hormuz after Iran blockade
UN to vote on Hormuz resolution as China opposes authorization of force
Iran to allow safe passage of Philippine ships, fuel supply through Strait of Hormuz, says Manila
French-owned CMA CGM container ship passes Strait of Hormuz, data shows
Near-term oil prices hit record premium over later deliveries after Trump vows more attacks on Iran
OPEC+ likely to weigh further oil output hike on Sunday, sources say
Israel strikes Beirut, US warns Iran may hit Lebanese universities
Saudi crude exports from Yanbu approach capacity in Hormuz workaround
Iran war puts Middle East Dubai oil benchmark under stress as prices soar
US LNG exports break record high as Middle East war disrupts global supply
Kenya tea exports hit by Iran conflict as stocks pile up

events.title: U.S. intelligence assesses Iran unlikely to ease Strait of Hormuz chokehold soon
events.implication: Signals prolonged chokepoint disruption; keeps oil/LNG freight and insurance elevated.

events.title: Iran uses attacks, mines and passage fees to effectively block Hormuz transit, Reuters reports
events.implication: Physical and economic barriers reinforce de facto closure; raises risk of further incidents at sea.

events.title: Iran shoots down U.S. F-15E over southwestern Iran; one crew member missing
events.implication: Downing of U.S. aircraft increases escalation and retaliation risk, elevating threat to regional assets.

events.title: Drone strike hits Mina al-Ahmadi refinery; fires reported, no injuries
events.implication: Adds tail risk to Gulf refining capacity and product exports; supports higher crack spreads.

events.title: Iranian fire hits U.S. A-10; aircraft crashes over Kuwait after engagement
events.implication: Highlights risk to coalition air operations and bases in Gulf states; may trigger additional strikes.

events.title: Iran strikes power and water plant in Kuwait, Reuters reports
events.implication: Utilities/desalination vulnerability raises broader Gulf stability risk beyond energy assets.

events.title: Iranian fire hits two U.S. Blackhawks during missing pilot search; helicopters exit Iran airspace
events.implication: Search-and-rescue operations face elevated attrition risk; may expand U.S. target set.

events.title: Drone hits Red Crescent relief warehouse in Choghadak, Bushehr province
events.implication: Signals expanding strike set inside Iran; raises risk of further asymmetric retaliation.

events.title: Britain says ~40 countries discuss coalition options to reopen Strait of Hormuz after blockade
events.implication: Coalition planning (incl. mine-clearing/reassurance force) could reduce shipping risk but depends on end of hostilities.

events.title: UN Security Council to vote on Bahrain-drafted resolution to protect commercial shipping around Hormuz
events.location_name: United Nations Headquarters, New York, United States
events.implication: If passed, could enable expanded maritime protection; great-power disagreement raises uncertainty and delays.

events.title: China opposes UNSC language authorizing 'all defensive means necessary' in Hormuz resolution
events.location_name: United Nations Headquarters, New York, United States
events.implication: Increases odds of watered-down mandate; reduces probability of rapid, force-backed reopening framework.

events.title: Philippines says Iran assures safe passage for Philippine-flagged vessels and energy supplies through Hormuz
events.implication: Shows Iran may allow selective transits, but does not resolve broader insurance and security constraints.

events.title: French-owned CMA CGM container ship Kribi transits Strait of Hormuz after signaling 'Owner France'
events.implication: Demonstrates possible deconfliction/neutrality signaling; may encourage limited traffic but doesn’t normalize flows.

events.title: WTI front-month backwardation hits record as traders scramble for prompt barrels
events.implication: Signals acute near-term tightness; raises volatility and margin/collateral needs for hedgers.

events.title: OPEC+ likely to weigh further output hike contingent on Hormuz reopening
events.implication: Adds ‘paper’ supply optionality, but physical flows depend on chokepoint access; supports front-end volatility.

events.title: Iran's Revolutionary Guard says it began attacks on Amazon cloud computing facility in Bahrain (ISNA report cited by Reuters)
events.implication: Raises regional cyber escalation risk and potential business interruption for cloud-dependent operations.

events.title: Israel strikes Beirut after warning residents to flee; U.S. warns Iran-aligned groups may target Lebanese universities
events.implication: Sustains multi-theatre conflict risk; increases probability of further regional spillover and risk-off moves.

events.title: Saudi crude exports from Yanbu approach capacity as kingdom reroutes to bypass Hormuz
events.implication: Bypass flows partially mitigate supply loss but are capacity-capped; supports persistent price and freight pressure.

events.title: Iran war strains Dubai oil benchmark as Hormuz export halts disrupt deliverable supply
events.implication: Benchmark/basis risk rises; Asian refiners face hedging and margin stress; may shift pricing to Brent-based benchmarks.

events.title: US LNG exports hit record high in March as Middle East war disrupts global supply
events.implication: Global gas rebalancing accelerates; higher Asia prices pull flexible cargoes, tightening tanker availability.

events.title: Kenya tea exports hit by Iran conflict-linked shipping disruption as stocks pile up in Mombasa
events.implication: Illustrates non-energy trade impacts (delays, inventory build); adds to EM macro stress where export earnings fall.

events.title: Vessel traffic through Strait of Hormuz nearly halted; large backlog of tankers reported
events.implication: Near-cessation of traffic amplifies global supply shock; raises probability of rationing and emergency releases.

events.title: IEA warns Middle East oil disruptions set to rise in April as Hormuz closure hits supply
events.implication: Official warning reinforces expectation of sustained supply shortfall; supports higher oil volatility.

events.title: Iran strikes fully loaded oil tanker at anchorage off Dubai, Reuters reports
events.implication: Tankers at anchor and port approaches become higher-risk; increases freight premiums and potential force majeure.

events.title: OPEC output plunges in March as Hormuz closure forces export cuts
events.implication: Production/export reductions deepen near-term supply tightness; supports backwardation and inventory draws.

events.title: Iran warns it will broaden attacks in response to U.S. threats, Reuters reports
events.implication: Escalatory messaging increases headline-driven volatility and raises probability of attacks on third-country assets.

events.title: More than 1 million tons of U.S. LNG idles or signals near Suez Canal amid rerouting
events.implication: Rerouting/idle behavior tightens tanker availability and can amplify regional price spreads.

events.title: Asian refiners switch from Dubai to Brent-based benchmarks for pricing U.S. crude amid dislocation
events.implication: Pricing regime shift can alter hedge correlations and basis; may raise volatility in Brent spreads.

events.title: Middle East crude prices surge toward $170/bbl as Hormuz loading constraints intensify, Reuters reports
events.implication: Higher absolute prices amplify inflation shock and margin stress for refiners; increases risk of demand destruction.

events.title: Macron says opening Strait of Hormuz by force is unrealistic; reopening must be via diplomacy
events.implication: Signals limited appetite for military seizure; implies reopening likely slower and dependent on negotiation.

links.from: U.S. intelligence assesses Iran unlikely to ease Strait of Hormuz chokehold soon
links.why: Prolonged chokepoint constraint supports a sustained oil risk premium and higher delivered costs.

links.from: U.S. intelligence assesses Iran unlikely to ease Strait of Hormuz chokehold soon
links.why: Persistent disruption plus escalation risk increases demand for safe havens and reduces risk appetite.

links.from: Drone strike hits Mina al-Ahmadi refinery; fires reported, no injuries
links.why: Direct attacks on Gulf energy infrastructure raise perceived probability of supply and product disruptions.

links.from: Iran shoots down U.S. F-15E over southwestern Iran; one crew member missing
links.why: Downed aircraft and rescue operations can widen the conflict and increase risk premia across assets.

links.from: U.S. intelligence assesses Iran unlikely to ease Strait of Hormuz chokehold soon
links.why: Higher energy-driven inflation risk and risk-off conditions typically lift term premia and widen spreads.

links.from: U.S. intelligence assesses Iran unlikely to ease Strait of Hormuz chokehold soon
links.why: Oil price spikes worsen trade balances for importers and can weaken EM FX, especially where subsidies strain budgets.

links.from: WTI front-month backwardation hits record as traders scramble for prompt barrels
links.why: Extreme backwardation reflects near-term scarcity expectations tied to disrupted Middle East flows.

links.from: US LNG exports hit record high in March as Middle East war disrupts global supply
links.why: Higher LNG prices and competition for flexible cargoes can stress energy-importing EMs through larger import bills.

links.from: UN Security Council to vote on Bahrain-drafted resolution to protect commercial shipping around Hormuz
links.to: U.S. intelligence assesses Iran unlikely to ease Strait of Hormuz chokehold soon
links.why: Diplomatic effort is a direct response to disruption and aims to create a protection/reopening framework.

links.from: Britain says ~40 countries discuss coalition options to reopen Strait of Hormuz after blockade
links.to: French-owned CMA CGM container ship Kribi transits Strait of Hormuz after signaling 'Owner France'
links.why: Coalition/diplomatic efforts coexist with ad-hoc neutrality signaling enabling limited transits.
